metadata.title: States PCS: Uttar Pradesh GK Questions for UPPSC Examinations

questions.question: Who ruled Mathura as Great Satrap around 10 CE?
questions.explanation: Rajuvula was the Indo-Scythian Great Satrap of Mathura around 10 CE, succeeding Indo-Greek rule. He was succeeded by his son Sodasa. His inscriptions are an important source for early Kushan period history.

questions.question: Who led Nagari Pracharini Sabha to promote Hindi and Devanagari script in Uttar Pradesh?
questions.explanation: Pandit Madan Mohan Malaviya led Nagari Pracharini Sabha, promoted Hindi and Devanagari script, pushed Hindi’s use in education and courts, and launched the influential journal Saraswati in Uttar Pradesh, significantly influencing the Hindi movement.

questions.question: Who was the famous tawaif and ruler of Sardhana in Uttar Pradesh?
questions.explanation: Begum Samru, originally a tawaif, became the ruler of Sardhana near Meerut, Uttar Pradesh. She commanded a mercenary army and was influential in Indo-European politics during the late 18th and early 19th centuries. Her palace at Sardhana is now a historical church. She converted to Catholicism.

questions.question: Which Vaishnav Bairagi sect, founded by Ramananda, was allotted one-third of the disputed land in the Ayodhya case by the Allahabad High Court in 2010?
questions.explanation: Nirmohi Akhara, founded by Ramananda, claimed ancient worship rights and was allotted one-third of the Ayodhya disputed land by the Allahabad High Court in 2010.

questions.question: Which city serves as the starting point of the Eastern Dedicated Freight Corridor within the ADKIC industrial corridor?
questions.explanation: The Eastern Dedicated Freight Corridor begins at Allahabad, a key city in the ADKIC spanning seven states in Uttar Pradesh.

questions.question: Who was the businessman implicated in the 2006 Noida serial murders case in Uttar Pradesh?
questions.explanation: Moninder Singh Pandher, a businessman from Noida, was involved along with servant Surinder Koli in the 2006 serial murders case.

questions.question: Consider the following pairs regarding medieval rulers and their corresponding contributions to Uttar Pradesh: Akbar – Built Fatehpur Sikri as capital Sikandar Lodi – Founded the city of Agra Ibrahim Sharqi – Commissioned Atala Masjid Aurangzeb – Reimposed Jizya on non-Muslims Which of the above pairs are correctly matched?
questions.explanation: All four pairs are correctly matched: Akbar established Fatehpur Sikri as his capital in the 16th century. Sikandar Lodi founded Agra in the late 15th century. Ibrahim Sharqi commissioned the Atala Masjid in Jaunpur. Aurangzeb infamously reimposed the Jizya tax on non-Muslims in his reign. These events are well-documented aspects of the medieval history of Uttar Pradesh.

questions.question: What are the approximate east–west length and north–south breadth of Uttar Pradesh, respectively?
questions.explanation: The east–west length of Uttar Pradesh is about 650 km, and its north–south breadth is about 240 km.

questions.question: In Uttar Pradesh, which soil type, due to its high pH and sodium content, cannot be rejuvenated naturally without artificial nutrient supplementation and shows poor response to organic manuring?
questions.explanation: Usar soil in Uttar Pradesh is saline-alkaline with high sodium and pH; it cannot recover without artificial supplements like gypsum and reacts poorly to organic manure.

questions.question: Match the following Bird Sanctuaries with their respective locations: Bakhira Bird Sanctuary Okhla Bird Sanctuary Patna Bird Sanctuary Parvati Aranga Bird Sanctuary Sanctuary locations: Sant Kabir Nagar Gautam Buddh Nagar Etah Gonda Which of the following pairs is/are correctly matched?
questions.explanation: Bakhira Bird Sanctuary is in Sant Kabir Nagar district, Okhla Bird Sanctuary in Gautam Buddh Nagar (Noida), Patna Bird Sanctuary in Etah, and Parvati Aranga Bird Sanctuary in Gonda. The correct order is A-1, B-2, C-3, D-4. Each sanctuary is an important conservation site known for rich avifaunal diversity and wetland ecosystems.
